pokemon:poliwag.name: Poliwag
pokemon:poliwag.summary: Poliwag has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the Pokémon’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Poliwag has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the Pokémon’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming than trying to stand.
The swirl on its belly is its insides showing through the skin. It looks clearer after it eats.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
It is possible to see this POKéMON’s spiral innards right through its thin skin. However, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.
Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.
Because it is in­ ept at walking on its newly grown legs, it always swims around in water.
Because it is inept at walking on its newly grown legs, it always swims around in water.
Its newly grown legs prevent it from walking well. It appears to prefer swimming over walking.
The direction of the spiral on the belly differs by area. It is more adept at swimming than walking.
The direction of the spiral on the belly differs by area. It is more adept at swimming than walking.
It’s still not very good at walking. Its Trainers should train this Pokémon to walk every day.

Poliwag has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the Pokémon’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming than trying to stand.

POLIWAG has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the POKéMON’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.

POLIWAG has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the POKéMON’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.
In rivers with fast-flowing water, this Pokémon will cling to a rock by using its thick lips, which act like a suction cup.
The direction of its belly spiral differs by area. The equator is thought to have an effect on this.
The direction of its belly spiral differs by area. The equator is thought to have an effect on this.
The swirl on its belly is its internal organs showing through. If the swirl is tinged white, that means it’s affected by some disease.
For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.
The direction of the swirl on their stomachs differs depending on where they live. Poliwag aficionados can tell them apart at a glance.
Despite the danger, it wants to come up on land. So it does its best to waddle along, but when an enemy finds it, it rushes back to the water.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.
Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.
The direction of the spiral on the belly differs by area. It is more adept at swimming than walking.
The direction of the spiral on the belly differs by area. It is more adept at swimming than walking.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Poliwag has a very thin skin. It is possible to see the Pokémon’s spiral innards right through the skin. Despite its thinness, however, the skin is also very flexible. Even sharp fangs bounce right off it.

pokemon:poliwhirl.name: Poliwhirl
pokemon:poliwhirl.summary: The surface of Poliwhirl’s body is always wet and slick with a slimy fluid. Because of this slippery covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: The surface of Poliwhirl’s body is always wet and slick with a slimy fluid. Because of this slippery covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.
Though it is skilled at walk­ ing, it prefers to live underwater where there is less danger.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
Its body surface is always wet and slick with an oily fluid. Because of this greasy covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
Its two legs are well developed. Even though it can live on the ground, it prefers living in water.
The swirl on its belly subtly un­ dulates. Staring at it may gradual­ ly cause drowsi­ ness.
The swirl on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it may gradually cause drowsiness.
It can live in or out of water. When out of water, it constantly sweats to keep its body slimy.
Under attack, it uses its belly spiral to put the foe to sleep. It then makes its escape.
Under attack, it uses its belly spiral to put the foe to sleep. It then makes its escape.
It marches over the land in search of bug Pokémon to eat. Then it takes them underwater so it can dine on them where it’s safe.
The surface of Poliwhirl’s body is always wet and slick with a slimy fluid. Because of this slippery covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.
The surface of POLIWHIRL’s body is always wet and slick with an oily fluid. Because of this greasy covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
The surface of POLIWHIRL’s body is always wet and slick with an oily fluid. Because of this greasy covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.
This Pokémon’s sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies’ grasp and escape.
The skin on most of its body is moist. However, the skin on its belly spiral feels smooth.
The skin on most of its body is moist. However, the skin on its belly spiral feels smooth.
Although it can live on land, it prefers to stay in the water, where it has fewer natural enemies.
Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.
Its health suffers when its skin dries out, so be sure to moisturize it diligently.
Although it has become capable of living on land, it spends its time in the water, where its prey, fish Pokémon, are plentiful.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.
It can live in or out of water. When out of water, it constantly sweats to keep its body slimy.
Its two legs are well developed. Even though it can live on the ground, it prefers living in water.
Under attack, it uses its belly spiral to put the foe to sleep. It then makes its escape.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: The surface of Poliwhirl’s body is always wet and slick with a slimy fluid. Because of this slippery covering, it can easily slip and slide out of the clutches of any enemy in battle.

pokemon:poliwrath.name: Poliwrath
pokemon:poliwrath.summary: Poliwrath’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this Pokémon can swim back and forth across the ocean without effort.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Poliwrath’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this Pokémon can swim back and forth across the ocean without effort.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breast stroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.
It can use its well-developed arms and legs to run on the surface of the water for a split second.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
Its highly developed muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. This POKéMON can swim back and forth across the Pacific Ocean without effort.
An adept swimmer, it knows the front crawl, butterfly, and more. It is faster than the best human swimmers.
This strong and skilled swimmer is even capable of crossing the Pacific Ocean just by kicking.
This strong and skilled swimmer is even capable of crossing the Pacific Ocean just by kicking.
A swimmer adept at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.
Swims powerfully using all the muscles in its body. It can even overtake world-class swimmers.
Swims powerfully using all the muscles in its body. It can even overtake world-class swimmers.
Its percentage of body fat is nearly zero. Its body is entirely muscle, which makes it heavy and forces its swimming prowess to develop.
Poliwrath’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this Pokémon can swim back and forth across the ocean without effort.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breast stroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.
POLIWRATH’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this POKéMON can swim back and forth across the Pacific Ocean without effort.
POLIWRATH’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this POKéMON can swim back and forth across the Pacific Ocean without effort.
Poliwrath is skilled at both swimming and martial arts. It uses its well-trained arms to dish out powerful punches.
Although an ener­ getic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its mus­ cles, it lives on dry land.
Although an energetic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its muscles, it lives on dry land.
Poliwrath in the Alola region are strong swimmers that use the breaststroke. Many children learn to swim by imitating Poliwrath.
Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.
The muscles it has developed through swimming are thick and powerful. When it lands a square punch, it can turn huge boulders to dust.
It’s quite a gifted swimmer, even among Water-type Pokémon, but it normally spends its time on land.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.
A swimmer adept at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.
Swims powerfully using all the muscles in its body. It can even overtake champion swimmers.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Poliwrath’s highly developed, brawny muscles never grow fatigued, however much it exercises. It is so tirelessly strong, this Pokémon can swim back and forth across the ocean without effort.
